---
title: "Teaching"
---

My teaching is grounded in creating an inclusive, supportive learning environment where students feel empowered to ask questions and develop their own ideas. Across my courses, I incorporate student-led research projects that encourage students to collect, analyze, and interpret data on topics that are meaningful to them.

My approach to teaching was shaped by my experience as a high school English teacher through the U.S. Fulbright Program in South Korea, where I worked with students across a wide range of language backgrounds and developed student-centered, intrinsically motivating lessons.

Across undergraduate and graduate courses in geography and biology, I have emphasized hands-on, data-driven learning. Students engage with real-world ecological questions through field data collection, spatial analysis, and modeling, gaining experience using practical tools such as GIS and R. I am especially passionate about helping students interpret and visualize data, skills that are critical for understanding and addressing the environmental challenges we face.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Courses Taught

### San Diego State University

**Instructor of Record**\
**GEOG 370 – Conservation Science and Policy** *(new prep)*\
2024

**Guest Lecturer**\
**GEOG 570 – Environmental Conservation Practice**\
2025

**Co-Instructor**\
**GEOG 710 – Seminar in Physical Geography: Species Distribution Modeling**\
2024-2026

### University of California, Riverside

**Instructor of Record**\
**BIOL 166 – Global Change Biology** *(online)*\
2024

**Guest Lecturer**\
**BPSC 146 – Plant Ecology**\
2023

**Co-Instructor**\
**BPSC 244 – Species Distribution Modeling**\
2022

**Graduate Teaching Assistant**\
**BIOL 005C – Introductory Evolution and Ecology Lab**\
2021

### University of Tennessee, Knoxville

**Graduate Teaching Assistant**\
**GEOG 131 – Weather, Climate, and Climate Change**\
2018

### U.S. Fulbright Program, South Korea

**English Teacher**\
High school English as a Foreign Language (beginner, intermediate, and advanced)\
2016–2017
